programs.name: Medical Assistance (MAWD for seniors/disabled)
programs.what_it_does: Medical Assistance is Pennsylvania's Medicaid program providing health coverage including long-term care for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. It covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescription drugs, and other medical services.
programs.who_its_for: Pennsylvania residents aged 65+ or disabled with low income and limited assets meeting state eligibility criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers premiums, copays, and services up to full Medicaid benefits[7][2]

programs.name: Community HealthChoices (CHC)
programs.what_it_does: CHC is Pennsylvania's home and community-based services waiver program allowing seniors to receive long-term care services at home instead of nursing facilities. It includes personal care, adult day services, and home modifications.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ or dual eligibles needing nursing home level care but preferring to remain at home, with income/assets meeting Medicaid thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $50,000+ annually in services based on assessed needs[6]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and transportation. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits for participants.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as needing nursing home care, living in PACE service areas, able to live safely in community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all health and long-term care needs[2]

programs.name: Healthy Horizons (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Healthy Horizons helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income seniors. It includes Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) programs.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ with Medicare, low income (e.g., under $1,235/month single for QMB), and limited assets.
programs.benefit_value: Pays Part A/B premiums (up to $174.70/month Part B) and copays[2][7]

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly benefits via EBT card to buy healthy food. Administered by PA DHS through County Assistance Offices.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (higher income disregard) meeting federal poverty guidelines.

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help pay heating bills and offers crisis assistance for emergencies. It also connects to weatherization services.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors 60+, based on income up to 150% federal poverty level.
programs.benefit_value: Grants $200-1,000+ for heating, plus emergency aid[2][7]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency improvements like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs. Delivered through local agencies.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ in eligible households, prioritized via LIHEAP.
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades saving $300-500/year on energy bills[2]

programs.name: SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, Part D, and supplemental plans. PA's SHIP is administered through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries 65+, spouses, and caregivers in Pennsylvania.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling saving thousands in improper plan choices[6]

programs.name: Home-Delivered Meals (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals delivered to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often funded via Older Americans Act through AAAs.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, with priority for low-income.

programs.name: Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, counseling, and training to family caregivers of seniors. Includes financial assistance for relief services.
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of PA residents 60+ or 55+ with dementia/Alzheimer's.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $600/month respite reimbursement, plus training[6]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Subsidizes part-time community service jobs with training to help seniors gain employment skills. Pays minimum wage for 20 hours/week.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ with income ≤125% poverty level.
programs.benefit_value: $7.25+/hour wage for 20 hours/week, plus job placement[4]

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems. Delivered via AAAs and legal aid nonprofits.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal needs.
programs.benefit_value: Free representation/counseling worth thousands in services[2][4]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Operates statewide via AAAs.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living, and personal care homes in PA.
